modal.title: Snapshots
modal.description:
Only the note's text content is saved (not attachments or images). If an attachment or image was deleted, it cannot be recovered from a snapshot.
Created automatically on first open each day. "Take snapshot now" adds an extra snapshot without replacing today's. Up to 7 kept.
modal.empty_hint: No snapshot for now, but you can create one manually right away.
modal.new_note_empty_hint: No snapshot for now, but you can create one manually right away.

confirm.title: Confirmation
confirm.restore_message: Restore the note to the snapshot state? Current changes will be lost.
confirm.take_now_message: Create an additional snapshot with the current note content? Existing snapshots for today will be kept.

structured_export.title: Structured Export
structured_export.description: Export your notes organized in folders that match your Poznote folder structure. Each note will be saved in a directory that corresponds to its folder in Poznote, preserving the full hierarchy. Markdown files include YAML frontmatter with metadata.
structured_export.warning: Note: This export type is not designed for a perfect Poznote backup.

direct_copy_restore.step1: 1. Copy the file to the container:

individual_notes.title: Do you want to import HTML, Markdown, text files or ZIP archives?
individual_notes.description: Import individual notes (HTML, Markdown or ZIP) to a specific workspace and folder
individual_notes.frontmatter_warning: If an MD note contains a folder key in a front matter, this value will override the one selected below. You must therefore ensure first that the folder already exists, otherwise the note will be created at the root of the workspace.
individual_notes.zip_folders_description: If your ZIP contains folders, they will be automatically created as folders in Poznote, preserving their hierarchy (subfolders included).
individual_notes.files_info: Multiple files (max {{maxIndividualFiles}}) or single ZIP file (max {{maxZipFiles}} files). These limits can be changed,
individual_notes.description_html:
Import individual HTML, Markdown, text or JSON files, or a ZIP archive containing multiple files.

Title will be automatically created from filename (without extension), unless a title key is defined in a front matter for MD files.

Tasklists: .json files containing tasks arrays and .md files with checkbox syntax (- [ ] or - [x]) are automatically imported as tasklists.
individual_notes.helper: You can select multiple files at once (Maximum 50 files). Supported formats: .html, .md, .markdown, .txt, .json

import_confirm.title: Are you sure?
import_confirm.body: This will replace note titles, tags, search index and metadata, but not actual note content. This action cannot be undone.

complete_restore.title: Complete restore?
complete_restore.warning_large_html:
Warning: This file is {{size}}MB. Standard upload might be slow or fail for large files.

This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.
complete_restore.warning_html: Warning: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.
complete_restore.body_html: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.

import_notes.title: Import notes?
import_notes.body: This will import all HTML files from the ZIP. Files will be added to your existing notes collection.

import_attachments.title: Import attachments?
import_attachments.body: This will import all files from the ZIP to attachments folder. Files will be added to your existing attachments.

complete_restore_direct_copy.title: Complete restore (Direct copy)?
complete_restore_direct_copy.body_html: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.

import_individual_notes.title: Import individual notes?
import_individual_notes.body: This will import notes to Poznote workspace.

validation.invalid_date_range: The start date must be before or equal to the end date.

git_sync.title: {{provider}} Sync
git_sync.description: Synchronize your notes and attachments to GitHub or Forgejo. Files are stored as flat files with a metadata.json that preserves tags, folders, dates and attachments.
git_sync.warning:
Do not rely on this feature as your sole backup.
Regularly export your data using the backup feature in Settings.
git_sync.confirm_push: Push all notes and attachments to {{provider}}? Existing files will be updated and files no longer in Poznote will be removed from the repository.
git_sync.confirm_pull: Pull all notes and attachments from {{provider}}? Local data will be overwritten with the remote version. Notes not found on {{provider}} will be moved to trash.
git_sync.confirm_auto_pull: A new session started. Do you want to pull changes from {{provider}}?
git_sync.confirm_auto_pull_warning:
A new session started. Do you want to pull changes from {{provider}}?

Local notes not found on {{provider}} will be moved to trash.
git_sync.auto_pull_no_changes: Git pull complete. No changes found.

config.title: Configuration
config.hint: {{provider}} sync is disabled. An administrator can enable it in Settings > Admin Tools.
config.hint_enable: Git sync is disabled. An administrator can enable it in Settings > Admin Tools.

auto_sync.title: Automatic Synchronization
auto_sync.push_description: When leaving a note (switching notes or closing the tab), its latest version is automatically pushed to the Git repository, if it was modified during the session.
auto_sync.pull_description: Once per session, asks whether to pull from the Git repository. Updates existing notes, imports new ones, and moves to trash any local notes not found on the remote.

actions.title: Sync Actions
actions.home_hint: Push and Pull can be done from Dashboard.

push.title: Push to {{provider}}
push.description: Exports all your notes and attachments to {{provider}} as flat files. Updates existing files and removes files that no longer exist in Poznote.

pull.title: Pull from {{provider}}
pull.description: Imports all notes and attachments from {{provider}}. Overwrites local content and moves to trash any notes not present in the repository.

messages.push_success: Successfully pushed {{count}} notes, deleted {{deleted}} files ({{errors}} errors)
messages.pull_success: Successfully pulled {{pulled}} new notes, updated {{updated}} existing notes, deleted {{deleted}} notes ({{errors}} errors)

limitations.title: Synchronization Limitations
limitations.embedded_note: Embedded images are preserved in HTML but not displayed on {{provider}}

not_configured.title: {{provider}} Sync Not Configured
not_configured.description: To use {{provider}} synchronization, you need to configure your {{provider}} credentials in the .env file.
not_configured.step1: Create a {{provider}} Personal Access Token at with 'repo' scope
not_configured.step2: Create a {{provider}} repository (public or private) for your notes
not_configured.step3: Add the configuration to your .env file
not_configured.step4: Restart the application for changes to take effect

orphan_scanner.title: Orphan attachments scanner
orphan_scanner.description: This tool scans your attachment folders and identifies files that are no longer referenced in any of your notes (including inline images, which are stored as attachments).
orphan_scanner.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete these files?

maintenance.title: Rebuild Master Database
maintenance.subtitle: Restore user profiles and public sharing links
maintenance.description: The main database (master.db) stores user information, their public links, and Poznote's global settings. If this file is lost or corrupted, or if some public links stop working after a restore, this tool scans your 'data/users' folders to automatically re-register accounts, recover their real names from their personal databases, and restore all public sharing links.
maintenance.repair_registry_confirm: This will scan all user folders and rebuild the master database. Continue?
maintenance.repair_registry_success:
Master database rebuilt successfully:

- {{scanned}} folders scanned
- {{added}} users restored
- {{links}} shared links rebuilt

oidc_admin.error_csrf: Invalid form submission. Please try again.

fields.auto_create_users: Auto-create user profiles on first OIDC login
fields.disable_basic_auth: Disable HTTP Basic Auth for API

hints.provider_name: Display name shown on the login button (e.g. Google, Azure AD, Keycloak)
hints.issuer: The issuer URL of your OIDC provider (e.g. https://accounts.google.com)
hints.credentials: Client ID and Client Secret are managed in the .env file.
hints.discovery_url: Override the auto-discovery URL (leave empty to derive from issuer)
hints.redirect_uri: Custom redirect URI (leave empty to auto-generate)
hints.end_session_endpoint: IdP logout endpoint for RP-initiated logout (optional)
hints.post_logout_redirect_uri: Where to redirect after logout (default: login page)
hints.groups_claim: Token claim name containing user groups (default: groups)
hints.allowed_groups: Comma-separated list of allowed groups. Leave empty to allow all authenticated users.
hints.auto_create_users: Recommended when using group-based access control
hints.allowed_users: Comma-separated emails/usernames. Leave empty to allow all authenticated users.
hints.api_audience: Accepted aud claim for OIDC Bearer JWT API requests. Leave empty to use the Client ID. Use commas for multiple audiences.
hints.disable_normal_login: When enabled, only OIDC login is available on the login page
hints.disable_basic_auth: When enabled, API requests with HTTP Basic Auth will be rejected (403)

smtp_admin.title: SMTP Configuration
smtp_admin.error_csrf: Invalid form submission. Please try again.

hints.enabled: When enabled, Poznote can use this SMTP provider to send emails.
hints.security_summary: STARTTLS usually uses port 587 and upgrades to TLS after connecting. SSL/TLS is encrypted from the connection, usually on port 465. None uses no encryption.
hints.security_tls: STARTTLS: standard connection, then upgrade to TLS, usually port 587.
hints.security_ssl: SSL/TLS: encrypted from the connection, usually port 465.
hints.security_none: None: no encryption, only for a local relay or trusted network.
hints.password_configured: A password is configured. Leave blank to keep it.
hints.app_url: Used to include a direct note link in reminder emails. This value is detected automatically; edit it if your public URL is different.
hints.test_recipient: Use this to verify the provider before enabling the SMTP configuration.

test.success: SMTP configuration saved and test email sent to the email address {{email}} configured for your account by the administrator.
test.error: SMTP configuration saved, but the test email failed: {{error}}
test.no_account_email: Your account does not have an email address configured. Contact an administrator to add one to your profile.
test.subject: Poznote SMTP test
test.body_text:
This is a test email from Poznote.

If you received it, your SMTP configuration works.
test.body_html_intro: This is a test email from Poznote.
test.body_html_success: If you received it, your SMTP configuration works.

note_lock.current_user_other_tab: Read only: this note is already being edited in another tab or on another device by the same user.
note_lock.unavailable: Read only: unable to verify edit access for this note.
note_lock.lost_to_other_tab: You no longer hold the edit lock for this note. It is now being edited in another tab.
note_lock.lost_to_user: You no longer hold the edit lock for this note. {{user}} is now editing it.

dashboard.title: Notes board
dashboard.empty: No favorite notes yet. Mark notes as favorites to pin them to this board.
dashboard.filter_placeholder: Filter by title, content or tag...

graph.title: Note graph
graph.empty_hint: Link notes together with [[Note Title]] to see connections here.
graph.show_orphans_hint: Show notes that have no links
graph.show_labels_hint: Show note titles under the dots

broken.tooltip: Note not found — click to create it

ai_settings.title: AI Assistant
ai_settings.description: Connect Poznote to an OpenAI-compatible AI server (Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, ...) to chat with an assistant about your notes.
ai_settings.enable_description: Shows an AI chat button in the note toolbar.
ai_settings.url_description: Base URL of an OpenAI-compatible API. For Ollama running on the Docker host, use http://host.docker.internal:11434
ai_settings.api_key_description: Leave empty for a local Ollama server. Required for cloud providers.
ai_settings.test_success: Connection successful. {{count}} model(s) available. Click a model below and save.
ai_settings.footer_note: The AI server is called from the Poznote server, not from your browser. Chat conversations are sent to the configured server; with a local Ollama instance nothing leaves your machine.
ai_settings.model_description: Use "Test connection" to list the models available on the server.

ai_chat.title: AI Assistant
ai_chat.empty:
Ask a question.
The assistant can search, read, create, rename and edit your notes.
ai_chat.no_tools_notice: This model does not support tools — the assistant cannot browse your notes.

diary.title: Diary
diary.today_button_title: Open today's entry (create it if needed)
diary.empty: No diary entries yet. Click "Today's entry" to write your first one. It will be stored in {{folder}}.
diary.create_error: Could not create the diary entry.
